phases.name: Data Persistence Layer
phases.description: Connect all services to PostgreSQL. This is the #1 blocker — all data is lost on restart.

phases.name: Core Exchange Hardening
phases.description: Fill functional gaps in matching, clearing, margin, settlement — the trading pipeline.

phases.name: Fee Management & Reference Data
phases.description: New services for fee calculation and instrument/commodity reference data — essential for real exchange ops.

phases.name: Risk Management & Surveillance
phases.description: Pre-trade risk checks, position limits, market surveillance — regulatory must-haves.

phases.name: Reporting & Participant Management
phases.description: Regulatory reporting, participant tiering, settlement statements.

phases.name: Production Infrastructure
phases.description: Real Kafka wiring, distributed sessions/rate-limiting, observability, secrets management.

phases.name: UI Refinement
phases.description: Trading UI order types, admin surveillance dashboards, mobile responsiveness.

phases.name: Integration Testing & Hardening
phases.description: E2E workflow tests, load testing, failure scenario testing.

tasks.title: Database connection layer — shared PostgreSQL client package
tasks.description:
Create a shared internal/db package used by all services. Implements connection pooling (pgxpool), health checks, migration runner, and transaction helpers. Each service will import this package.

Deliverables:
- src/shared/internal/db/pool.go — pgxpool wrapper with config from env (DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DB_NAME, DB_SSL_MODE, DB_MAX_CONNS)
- src/shared/internal/db/migrate.go — golang-migrate runner for embedded SQL files
- src/shared/internal/db/tx.go — transaction helper (begin, commit, rollback)
- src/shared/internal/db/health.go — ping-based health check
- Tests with dockertest or testcontainers

Acceptance: Package compiles, connects to PostgreSQL, runs migrations, executes queries in transactions.

tasks.title: Auth service — PostgreSQL persistence (users, sessions, API keys)
tasks.description:
Replace auth-service in-memory store with PostgreSQL. Write migration V10__auth_tables.sql creating: auth.users, auth.sessions, auth.api_keys, auth.pkce_challenges, auth.audit_log. Implement store/postgres.go implementing the existing Store interface using pgxpool.

Key tables:
- users: id, email, password_hash, role, locked_until, failed_attempts, created_at, updated_at
- sessions: id, user_id, refresh_token_hash, expires_at, revoked, created_at
- api_keys: id, user_id, key_hash, name, permissions, expires_at, revoked
- audit_log: id, user_id, action, ip_address, user_agent, created_at

Update main.go to use PostgreSQL store when DB_HOST is set, fallback to in-memory for tests.

Acceptance: All existing 43 auth tests still pass. New integration test connects to real PostgreSQL.

tasks.title: Matching engine — PostgreSQL persistence (orders, trades, execution reports)
tasks.description:
Add PostgreSQL persistence to matching-engine for trade history and execution reports. The order book stays in-memory (performance), but all trades and execution reports are persisted.

Migration V11__matching_engine_tables.sql:
- exchange.orders: id, instrument_id, account_id, side, type, tif, price, quantity, filled_qty, status, created_at, updated_at
- exchange.trades: id, instrument_id, buy_order_id, sell_order_id, price, quantity, buyer_id, seller_id, aggressor_side, traded_at
- exchange.execution_reports: id, order_id, trade_id, exec_type, status, price, quantity, leaves_qty, cum_qty, created_at

Implement async trade writer (channel-buffered) so matching latency is not affected by DB writes.

Acceptance: Matching engine tests pass. Trades persist across service restart.

tasks.title: Clearing engine — PostgreSQL persistence (obligations, positions, netting)
tasks.description:
Replace clearing-engine in-memory stores with PostgreSQL.

Migration V12__clearing_engine_tables.sql:
- clearing.obligations: id, trade_id, buyer_id, seller_id, instrument_id, price, quantity, status, ccp_id, created_at
- clearing.positions: id, participant_id, instrument_id, net_qty, avg_price, realized_pnl, updated_at (UPSERT on clear)
- clearing.netting_results: id, run_id, participant_id, instrument_id, net_payable, net_receivable, created_at

Implement store/postgres.go for ObligationStore, PositionStore interfaces.

Acceptance: All clearing tests pass. Positions persist across restart.

tasks.title: Margin engine — PostgreSQL persistence + real collateral source
tasks.description:
Replace margin-engine in-memory stores with PostgreSQL AND implement real collateral lookup from clearing-engine positions.

Migration V13__margin_engine_tables.sql:
- margin.portfolio_margins: id, participant_id, initial_margin, maintenance_margin, collateral_value, excess_deficit, calculated_at
- margin.margin_calls: id, participant_id, call_amount, status, deadline, issued_at, resolved_at
- margin.margin_parameters: instrument_id, initial_margin_pct, maintenance_margin_pct, updated_at

Collateral source: HTTP call to clearing-engine /positions endpoint to get net position value as proxy for collateral. Later will integrate with bank/treasury.

Acceptance: Margin calculations use real position data. Margin calls persist.

tasks.title: Settlement engine — PostgreSQL persistence + real position lookup
tasks.description:
Replace settlement-engine in-memory stores with PostgreSQL.

Migration V14__settlement_engine_tables.sql:
- settlement.cycles: id, status, valuation_time, total_payin, total_payout, started_at, completed_at
- settlement.instructions: id, cycle_id, participant_id, amount, direction, status, created_at, confirmed_at
- settlement.prices: instrument_id, settlement_price, price_date

Integrate with clearing-engine for position data and market-data-service for settlement prices.

Acceptance: Settlement cycles persist. P&L calculations use real positions.

tasks.title: Compliance service — PostgreSQL persistence (KYC, screening, alerts)
tasks.description:
Replace compliance-service in-memory stores with PostgreSQL.

Migration V15__compliance_tables.sql (completing V7 stub):
- compliance.kyc_applications: id, participant_id, legal_name, entity_type, status, submitted_at, reviewed_at, reviewer_id
- compliance.documents: id, application_id, doc_type, file_path, uploaded_at
- compliance.screening_results: id, participant_id, provider, status, matches_found, screened_at
- compliance.alerts: id, participant_id, alert_type, severity, status, created_at, resolved_at
- compliance.audit_log: id, actor_id, action, target_id, details_json, created_at
- compliance.sar_filings: id, participant_id, reason, filed_at, reference_number

Acceptance: KYC workflow persists. Screening results are queryable.

tasks.title: Market data service — TimescaleDB persistence (candles, tickers, trades)
tasks.description:
Replace market-data-service in-memory stores with TimescaleDB (PostgreSQL extension already available via timescale/timescaledb Docker image).

Migration V16__market_data_tables.sql (completing V8 stub):
- market_data.trades: time (TIMESTAMPTZ), instrument_id, price, quantity, buyer_id, seller_id, trade_id (hypertable, partitioned by time)
- market_data.candles: time, instrument_id, interval, open, high, low, close, volume, vwap (hypertable)
- market_data.tickers: instrument_id, bid, ask, last, volume_24h, vwap_24h, updated_at

Use TimescaleDB continuous aggregates for candle rollup (1m → 5m → 15m → 1h → 4h → 1d).
Retention policies: 30d for 1m candles, 1yr for 1d candles.

Acceptance: Trade ingestion persists. Historical candle queries work.

tasks.title: Warehouse service — PostgreSQL persistence (connect existing V9 migration)
tasks.description:
Warehouse service already has V9__warehouse_tables.sql with full schema. Connect it.

Implement store/postgres.go for FacilityStore, ReceiptStore, InspectionStore, DeliveryStore, InventoryStore interfaces using pgxpool.

This is the simplest DB integration since the schema already exists — just wire the store layer.

Acceptance: All warehouse tests pass. Receipts and inventory persist.

tasks.title: Reference data service — instrument definitions, commodities, delivery months
tasks.description:
Create a new reference-data-service (or extend matching-engine config) to manage instrument definitions in PostgreSQL instead of hardcoded env vars.

Migration V17__reference_data_tables.sql:
- reference.commodities: id, name, category (grain, oilseed, livestock, dairy), unit, grade_specs_json
- reference.instruments: id, commodity_id, delivery_month, delivery_year, contract_size, tick_size, min_price_increment, trading_hours, first_trade_date, last_trade_date, delivery_start, delivery_end, settlement_type (physical/cash), status (active/expired/suspended)
- reference.delivery_locations: id, name, lat, lng, address, capacity, commodities_accepted[]
- reference.trading_calendar: date, is_trading_day, market_open, market_close, notes

Seed data: WHT-HRW-2026M07-UB definition + 5 more agricultural contracts (corn, soybeans, barley, cashmere, livestock).

Gateway route: GET /api/v1/instruments (list), GET /api/v1/instruments/{id} (detail), GET /api/v1/commodities.

Acceptance: Instruments queryable from DB. Matching engine reads instrument config from DB on startup.

tasks.title: Fee management service — trading fees, clearing fees, fee schedules
tasks.description:
Create fee calculation logic (can be a package in the gateway or a standalone service).

Migration V18__fee_tables.sql:
- fees.fee_schedules: id, name, effective_from, effective_to, status
- fees.fee_rules: id, schedule_id, fee_type (trading/clearing/data/membership), instrument_pattern, participant_tier, rate_bps, min_fee, max_fee, per_contract_fee
- fees.fee_transactions: id, trade_id, participant_id, fee_type, amount, currency, created_at
- fees.participant_tiers: participant_id, tier (farmer/hedger/speculator/market_maker), volume_30d, updated_at

Fee model for agricultural exchange:
- Farmers/hedgers: 10 bps round-turn
- Professional traders: 20 bps
- Speculators: 25 bps
- Market makers: 5 bps (incentive)

Gateway routes: GET /api/v1/fees/schedule, GET /api/v1/fees/my-fees, GET /api/v1/admin/fees (CRUD).

Fee deduction: after trade execution, clearing engine calls fee service to calculate and record fees.

Acceptance: Fees calculated per trade. Fee schedule configurable via admin API.

tasks.title: Pre-trade risk checks — position limits, order size limits, margin pre-check
tasks.description:
Add pre-trade risk validation to the gateway before forwarding orders to matching engine.

Implement src/gateway/internal/risk/pretrade.go:
1. Position limit check: reject if new order would exceed participant's position limit for instrument
2. Order size limit: reject orders above max_order_qty per instrument
3. Price reasonability: reject limit orders >X% away from last trade (configurable band)
4. Margin pre-check: verify participant has sufficient margin headroom before accepting order
5. Participant status check: reject if participant is suspended/disabled

Migration V19__risk_parameters.sql:
- risk.position_limits: participant_id, instrument_id, max_long, max_short, max_gross
- risk.order_limits: instrument_id, max_order_qty, max_order_value, price_band_pct

Gateway integration: risk checks run BEFORE forwarding POST /api/v1/orders to matching engine.
Admin routes: PUT /api/v1/admin/risk/position-limits, GET /api/v1/admin/risk/position-limits.

Acceptance: Orders exceeding limits are rejected with clear error messages. Risk params configurable.

tasks.title: Market surveillance — trade pattern detection and alert generation
tasks.description:
Add basic market surveillance capabilities to compliance-service.

Implement surveillance rules:
1. Wash trading detection: same participant on both sides within time window
2. Spoofing detection: large orders cancelled within X seconds
3. Price manipulation: single participant causing >Y% price move
4. Concentration alert: participant holding >Z% of open interest
5. Unusual volume: participant trading >N standard deviations above their average

New package: src/compliance-service/internal/surveillance/
- rules.go: rule definitions with configurable thresholds
- detector.go: processes trade stream, applies rules, generates alerts
- alert.go: creates compliance alerts for review

Migration V20__surveillance_tables.sql:
- compliance.surveillance_rules: id, rule_type, parameters_json, enabled, created_at
- compliance.surveillance_alerts: id, rule_id, participant_id, instrument_id, severity, details_json, status, detected_at

Kafka consumer: subscribe to ace.trades.executed, run surveillance rules on each trade.

Gateway routes: GET /api/v1/compliance/surveillance/alerts, PUT /api/v1/admin/surveillance/rules.

Acceptance: Wash trading and spoofing detected in tests. Alerts generated and queryable.

tasks.title: Participant tiering and membership management
tasks.description:
Extend compliance-service with participant tiering (farmer, hedger, speculator, market_maker, clearing_member) and membership lifecycle.

Migration V21__participant_management.sql:
- participants.members: id, user_id, legal_name, entity_type, tier, status (active/suspended/terminated), onboarded_at, net_worth_category
- participants.membership_history: id, member_id, action, reason, actor_id, created_at
- participants.authorized_traders: id, member_id, trader_user_id, permissions[], active

Business logic:
- Tier determines: fee rate, position limits, order size limits, margin requirements
- Membership lifecycle: application → KYC approved → active → suspended/terminated
- Clearing member vs non-clearing member distinction
- Farmer groups: special tier allowing aggregated trading under single membership

Gateway routes: GET/PUT /api/v1/admin/participants/{id}/tier, GET /api/v1/admin/membership/stats.

Acceptance: Participant tier affects fee calculation and risk limits.

tasks.title: Settlement statements and regulatory reporting
tasks.description:
Add reporting capabilities to settlement-engine and a new reporting package.

Implement:
1. Daily settlement statement per participant: positions, margin, P&L, fees, net settlement amount
2. End-of-day report: market summary (volume, open interest, settlement prices, high/low)
3. Large trader report: positions above reporting threshold
4. Trade summary report: all trades for participant in date range

Migration V22__reporting_tables.sql:
- reporting.daily_statements: id, participant_id, report_date, positions_json, margin_json, pnl_json, fees_json, net_amount, generated_at
- reporting.market_summaries: id, instrument_id, report_date, open, high, low, close, volume, open_interest, settlement_price
- reporting.large_trader_positions: id, participant_id, instrument_id, report_date, net_position, gross_position, pct_of_oi

Gateway routes:
- GET /api/v1/reports/settlement-statement?date=YYYY-MM-DD (own statement)
- GET /api/v1/reports/trade-summary?from=&to= (own trades)
- GET /api/v1/admin/reports/market-summary?date= (admin)
- GET /api/v1/admin/reports/large-traders?date= (admin)

Acceptance: Settlement statements generated after each cycle. Market summaries queryable.

tasks.title: Real Kafka event streaming — replace channel stubs with wire protocol
tasks.description:
Wire real Kafka producer/consumer in all 9 services using segmentio/kafka-go.

Implement src/shared/internal/kafka/producer.go and consumer.go that satisfy the existing Producer/Consumer interfaces but use real Kafka wire protocol.

For each service's main.go:
- If KAFKA_BROKERS env is set → use real KafkaProducer/KafkaConsumer
- If not set → fallback to ChannelProducer/ChannelConsumer (tests)

Ensure:
- Automatic topic creation (or pre-create in create-topics.sh)
- Consumer groups per service (e.g., clearing-engine-group)
- At-least-once delivery with idempotency keys
- Dead letter queue for failed messages
- Graceful shutdown (flush pending messages)

Acceptance: With Docker Compose (Kafka running), trades flow from matching-engine → clearing-engine → margin-engine → settlement-engine via real Kafka.

tasks.title: Distributed session store (Redis) for auth and rate limiting
tasks.description:
Replace in-memory session storage and rate limiter state with Redis.

Implement:
1. src/auth-service/internal/store/redis_session.go — sessions in Redis with TTL
2. src/gateway/internal/middleware/redis_ratelimit.go — sliding window rate limiter using Redis INCR + EXPIRE

Redis connection via REDIS_URL env var (default: redis://localhost:6379).

Benefits:
- Sessions survive auth-service restart
- Rate limits shared across gateway replicas
- Token revocation immediate across all pods

Update docker-compose.yml: Redis already exists (ace-redis → garudax-redis).

Acceptance: Rate limiting works across 2 gateway instances. Sessions survive restart.

tasks.title: OpenTelemetry tracing and structured logging
tasks.description:
Add distributed tracing and consistent structured logging to all services.

Implement src/shared/internal/observability/:
- tracer.go: OpenTelemetry tracer provider (export to stdout/OTLP)
- logger.go: structured JSON logger (slog-based) with trace_id, service_name, request_id
- middleware.go: HTTP/gRPC interceptors that create spans and inject trace context

For each service:
- Add tracer initialization in main.go
- Replace log.Printf with structured logger
- Add span creation for key business operations (match, clear, settle)

Gateway: propagate trace-id header (X-Request-ID → W3C traceparent).

Acceptance: Request trace visible across gateway → matching-engine → clearing-engine via logs.

tasks.title: Matching engine — auction phases (pre-open, opening auction, closing auction)
tasks.description:
Implement proper market phases in matching engine.

Current: only CONTINUOUS + HALTED. Need full phase lifecycle:
1. PRE_OPEN: orders accepted but not matched (accumulate)
2. OPENING_AUCTION: uncrossing auction to determine opening price (maximize volume at single price)
3. CONTINUOUS: current price-time priority matching
4. CLOSING_AUCTION: uncrossing auction for settlement price
5. POST_CLOSE: no new orders, cancellations only

Implement:
- src/matching-engine/internal/orderbook/auction.go: auction price determination algorithm (max volume, min surplus, reference price tiebreak)
- Phase transition scheduling: configurable per instrument (e.g., pre-open 9:00-9:30, continuous 9:30-15:00, closing auction 15:00-15:05)
- Trading calendar integration: market holidays, early close days

Admin routes: POST /api/v1/admin/instruments/{id}/phase (manual phase transition).

Acceptance: Auction produces single equilibrium price. Phase transitions work on schedule.

tasks.title: Matching engine — additional order types (iceberg, GTD, stop-market improvements)
tasks.description:
Enhance order type support:

1. Iceberg orders: display_qty < total_qty, only display_qty visible on book, auto-replenish on fill
2. GTD (Good Till Date): orders with expiry timestamp, expired by scheduler
3. Stop-market improvement: current stop orders trigger immediately — add trigger price monitoring
4. OCO (One-Cancels-Other): paired orders where filling one cancels the other

Implement in src/matching-engine/internal/orderbook/:
- iceberg.go: iceberg order management
- scheduler.go: GTD expiry checker (runs every second)
- stop_monitor.go: monitors last trade price, triggers stop orders when price crosses

Acceptance: Iceberg orders show partial quantity. GTD orders expire. Stop orders trigger correctly.

tasks.title: Clearing engine — default management fund and waterfall
tasks.description:
Implement CCP default management waterfall per IOSCO/PFMI principles.

Default waterfall (when a clearing member fails to meet obligations):
1. Defaulting member's margin and collateral
2. Defaulting member's default fund contribution
3. CCP's own capital (skin-in-the-game)
4. Non-defaulting members' default fund contributions (mutualized)
5. CCP additional capital / insurance

Migration V23__default_fund.sql:
- clearing.default_fund_contributions: participant_id, amount, currency, deposited_at
- clearing.default_events: id, defaulting_participant_id, total_loss, waterfall_used_json, status, created_at
- clearing.ccp_capital: id, amount, purpose, deposited_at

Implement src/clearing-engine/internal/default_mgmt/:
- waterfall.go: executes loss allocation through tiers
- fund.go: manages default fund contributions

Acceptance: Default scenario test shows correct waterfall execution.

tasks.title: Margin engine — SPAN-like margining model with scenarios
tasks.description:
Replace simple percentage-based margin with scenario-based margin model.

Current: margin = position_value * margin_pct (flat %).
Target: SPAN-like scanning across price scenarios.

Implement src/margin-engine/internal/span/:
- scanner.go: 16-scenario risk array (price up/down x volatility up/down x 4 magnitudes)
- Calculate worst-case loss across scenarios as initial margin
- Inter-commodity spread credits (reduce margin for offsetting positions)
- Delivery month charge (higher margin as delivery approaches)

Migration V24__margin_scenarios.sql:
- margin.risk_arrays: instrument_id, scenario_id, price_shift_pct, vol_shift_pct, pnl_impact
- margin.spread_credits: long_instrument_id, short_instrument_id, credit_pct

Acceptance: Margin calculated via scenarios. Spread positions get margin reduction.

tasks.title: Settlement engine — DVP settlement and multi-instrument support
tasks.description:
Enhance settlement engine for delivery-vs-payment and multi-instrument operation.

Current gaps:
- Only single instrument support
- No DVP synchronization (delivery and payment happen independently)
- Payment gateway is stub

Implement:
1. Multi-instrument settlement: run cycle across all active instruments
2. DVP coordinator: ensures commodity delivery and cash payment happen atomically
3. Settlement price determination: use closing auction price or VWAP of last N trades
4. Settlement failure handling: if payment fails, retry with escalation to default waterfall

Update settlement cycle to:
1. VALUING: fetch settlement prices from market-data
2. CALCULATED: compute P&L per position per instrument
3. NETTING: net across instruments per participant
4. SETTLING: execute payments (DVP for physical, cash for financial)
5. COMPLETED/FAILED

Acceptance: Multi-instrument settlement cycle completes. DVP test passes.

tasks.title: Trading UI — advanced order entry, position management, trade history
tasks.description:
Enhance web-ui trading interface with production features.

Add:
1. Order type selector: Limit, Market, Stop-Limit, Stop-Market, Iceberg, OCO
2. Time-in-force selector: DAY, GTC, GTD (with date picker), IOC, FOK
3. Position panel: real-time P&L (unrealized), average entry price, close position button
4. Trade history tab: filterable by date, instrument, side with CSV export
5. Order modification: click on open order to modify price/qty
6. Depth chart: visual bid/ask depth (bar chart alongside order book)
7. Instrument info panel: contract specs, delivery month, tick size, position limits

Acceptance: All order types submittable. Positions show real-time P&L. Trade history exportable.

tasks.title: Admin UI — surveillance dashboard, fee management, reporting views
tasks.description:
Enhance admin-ui with new operational pages.

Add:
1. Surveillance dashboard: live alert feed, alert resolution workflow, rule configuration
2. Fee management page: view/edit fee schedules, participant tier assignment, fee transaction log
3. Reporting page: generate and download daily statements, market summaries, large trader reports
4. Participant detail page: full profile (tier, positions, margin, fee history, KYC status, alerts)
5. Market phase control: current phase indicator per instrument, manual phase transition buttons
6. Default fund dashboard: contribution levels, waterfall status

Acceptance: Surveillance alerts visible and resolvable. Fee schedules editable. Reports downloadable.

tasks.title: E2E workflow test — full trade lifecycle (order → match → clear → margin → settle)
tasks.description:
Create comprehensive end-to-end test that exercises the full trading lifecycle through real services.

Test flow:
1. Register 2 participants (trader A, trader B) + admin
2. KYC approval for both traders
3. Trader A submits BUY 10 @ 325.50
4. Trader B submits SELL 10 @ 325.50 → trade matched
5. Verify trade in matching engine
6. Verify clearing obligation created (novated through CCP)
7. Verify positions updated in clearing engine
8. Trigger margin calculation → verify margin requirements
9. Run settlement cycle → verify P&L and settlement instructions
10. Verify market data updated (last trade, candle, ticker)
11. Verify compliance audit trail
12. Verify fees calculated

Implement in tests/e2e/lifecycle_test.go.
Requires all services running (Docker Compose).

Acceptance: Full lifecycle test passes with real services. Data persists in PostgreSQL.

tasks.title: Load test — matching engine throughput and latency benchmarks
tasks.description:
Create load test suite for the matching engine to establish performance baselines.

Implement tests/load/matching_bench_test.go:
1. Single-instrument throughput: submit N orders/sec, measure match latency p50/p95/p99
2. Multi-instrument: concurrent orderbooks, verify no cross-contamination
3. Deep book stress: 10K resting orders, then aggressive sweep
4. Burst test: 1000 orders in 100ms, verify all processed correctly
5. Cancel storm: high cancel rate, verify book consistency

Targets for agricultural commodity exchange:
- Throughput: >1000 orders/sec per instrument
- Latency: <10ms p99 match latency
- Book integrity: zero lost or duplicate orders

Also test clearing-engine throughput (novation rate) and settlement-engine (cycle completion time).

Acceptance: Benchmarks documented. Latency within targets. No data corruption under load.

tasks.title: Secrets management — JWT key rotation, DB credentials from env/vault
tasks.description:
Harden secrets management across all services.

1. JWT signing: support RSA key pair (RS256) instead of HS256 shared secret
   - Generate RSA key pair, store private in auth-service, distribute public to all services
   - Support key rotation: accept tokens signed by current OR previous key
2. Database credentials: ensure all services read from env vars, no defaults
3. API keys: hash with bcrypt before storage (already done in auth)
4. Kafka credentials: SASL authentication when KAFKA_SASL_USER is set
5. Remove all hardcoded dev secrets from source code

Update docker-compose.yml: generate JWT RSA key on first run, mount as volume.
Update K8s manifests: reference Kubernetes secrets for all credentials.

Acceptance: No hardcoded secrets in code. JWT uses RS256. Key rotation test passes.

tasks.title: Warehouse receipt financing — pledge-as-collateral integration with margin
tasks.description:
Connect warehouse receipts to the margin system so physical commodity stored in certified warehouses can serve as collateral.

This is a KEY feature for agricultural commodity exchanges (ECX, EAX model).

Implement:
1. Warehouse service: when receipt is pledged, publish event to ace.warehouse.receipt-pledged with receipt value
2. Margin engine: consume pledge events, add receipt value to participant's collateral
3. Collateral valuation: receipt value = quantity * settlement_price * haircut (e.g., 80%)
4. Unpledge: when receipt is delivered or cancelled, reduce collateral

Gateway route: GET /api/v1/margin/collateral/{participant_id} → shows cash + receipt collateral.

Acceptance: Pledged warehouse receipt increases participant's margin headroom. Delivery reduces it.

tasks.title: Seed data enhancement — multiple instruments, realistic market simulation
tasks.description:
Enhance seed-data.sh to create a realistic exchange environment.

Seed:
1. 6 agricultural commodity instruments:
   - WHT-HRW-2026M07-UB (Hard Red Winter Wheat, July 2026)
   - CRN-YEL-2026M09-UB (Yellow Corn, Sept 2026)
   - SBN-NO2-2026M11-UB (No.2 Soybeans, Nov 2026)
   - BRL-MALT-2026M07-UB (Malting Barley, July 2026)
   - CSH-RAW-2026M09-UB (Raw Cashmere, Sept 2026)
   - LVS-CATTLE-2026M10-UB (Live Cattle, Oct 2026)
2. Participant tiers: 3 farmers, 2 hedgers, 2 speculators, 1 market maker, 1 admin
3. Simulate 50+ trades across instruments with realistic prices
4. Create order book depth (resting orders at various levels)
5. Warehouse receipts for wheat and barley
6. Fee schedule with tier-based pricing

Acceptance: After running seed script, all instruments have book depth, trade history, and market data.
